question: Will CPI rise more than 1.1% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. <cite index="2-1,14-1,14-7">The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis</cite>. Since 0.3% is significantly less than the 1.1% threshold required for YES resolution, this market will definitively resolve to NO. There is no uncertainty remaining - the official data is available and clearly shows March CPI rose only 0.3%, well below the 1.1% requirement.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing March 2026 CPI increase of 0.3%
0.3% is well below the 1.1% threshold required for YES resolution
Market closes tomorrow but outcome is already determined by released data
No possibility of data revision that could change the outcome
risks: Extremely minimal risk of data revision affecting single-decimal precision
Potential for misunderstanding of resolution criteria (though unlikely)
Market inefficiency keeping price above zero despite certain outcome
